instructions.heading: API Architecture
instructions.rule: Use Express.js with TypeScript. Implement middleware chain: logging, auth, validation, rate limiting, error handling. Keep route handlers thin - delegate to service layer.

instructions.heading: Route Organization
instructions.rule: Group routes by resource in separate files. Use express.Router() for each resource. Mount routers in main app.ts. Follow RESTful conventions: GET/POST/PUT/DELETE.

instructions.heading: Request Validation
instructions.rule: Validate request body, query params, and path params using Zod schemas. Return 400 with detailed validation errors. Never process invalid requests.

instructions.heading: Response Format
instructions.rule: Use consistent response format: { success: boolean, data?: any, error?: string }. Return appropriate HTTP status codes. Include request ID in all responses for tracing.

instructions.heading: Rate Limiting
instructions.rule: Implement rate limiting per endpoint and per user. Use Redis for distributed rate limiting. Return 429 with Retry-After header. Document rate limits in API docs.

instructions.heading: Database Access
instructions.rule: Use Prisma ORM for database access. Never expose Prisma models directly in API responses. Use DTO pattern to transform data. Implement connection pooling.

instructions.heading: Error Handling
instructions.rule: Implement centralized error handler middleware. Log all errors with context. Never expose internal errors to clients. Return sanitized error messages.
